title: RTI under threat? Why Anna Hazare is fighting Maharashtra’s new rules | Pulse Maharashtra
content: The Maharashtra government issued a notification amending certain provisions of the Maharashtra Right to Information Rules, 2026. The amendments have drawn criticism from social activists.

title: Digital Arrest scams: CBI conducts searches at over 80 locations across India
content: The arrested accused were involved in the incorporation of shell companies and the opening and operation of mule bank accounts. These accounts were reportedly used for laundering about ₹2 crore of suspected proceeds of crime.

title: In Telangana, the right to walk derailed by design
content: The Supreme Court declared the ‘right to walk on demarcated footpaths’ a fundamental right. In Hyderabad, however, decades of vehicle-centric road planning have left pedestrians at a dead end. Swathi Vadlamudi takes a closer look at the state of the city’s footpaths and the challenges of making the right a reality

title: CPI(M) faults govt.’s claims on reduction in power tariffs
content: It is misleading the public by highlighting marginal reductions in electricity charges after imposing larger financial burdens through previous revisions, says party State secretary Srinivasa Rao

title: Rahul Gandhi should apologise for Emergency: J.P. Nadda
content: He made the statement in Patna during the Samvidhan Hatya Diwas (Constitution Murder Day) programme organised by Bihar unit of the BJP, on the 51st anniversary of the ‘Emergency’ declared by   Indira Gandhi in 1975

title: Four held in Ujjain for blowing up car in Muharram stunt; police rule out communal angle
content: Viral videos show a white van hanging through a crane about 40 feet from the ground which is blown up with firecrackers kept inside it, creating a significant explosion

title: Turned city of joy into city of death: West Bengal CM blames Trinamool regime for warehouse tragedy
content: With Chief Minister Suvendu Adhikari blaming former Mayor Firhad Hakim who has aligned with the rebel group of Trinamool MLAs, Mamata Banerjee loyalists have sought the arrest of former Mayor.

title: FIR alleges assault on J&K police personnel by 40 Army men, including officers
content: “Army personnel climbed over the main gate and boundary wall and forcibly entered the police station premises,” say police; Army to extend “full cooperation” into the probe

title: Money laundering case: Actor Jacqueline Fernandez withdraws plea from Supreme Court
content: A Delhi court on May 30 ordered framing of charges against the actor, alleged conman Sukesh Chandrashekar and 15 others in the case

title: Health Ministry expands QR code-based drug traceability to vaccines, antimicrobials, and cancer medicines
content: Amended Drugs Rules bring vaccines, anti-cancer drugs, antimicrobials and NDPS medicines under Schedule H2; phased implementation to begin from July 2027

title: Passport application fee to go up from July 1
content: Aapplying for a fresh or reissued 36-page passport will cost ₹2,500, while a tatkal application will cost ₹5,000; rate for regular 60-page passports hiked to ₹3,500 and tatkal application will cost ₹6,000

title: In blockaded part of Manipur, an LPG cylinder now sells for ₹5,000
content: 40 people killed in Manipur since President’s Rule was revoked in February; Kangpokpi MLA and Deputy CM blocked from travel to Imphal; spike in prices of rice, petrol, no LPG cylinder supply in district

title: Three States have arrived at ‘historic consensus’, says D.K. Shivakumar as new gates of Tungabhadra dam are inaugurated
content: Shivakumar said Karnataka, Andhra Pradesh and Telangana discussed key concerns raised by farmers, including the proposed Navali balancing reservoir, and the need to remove accumulated silt from the reservoir to improve water conservation and storage

title: A 28-year-old’s killing woke up Bihar’s conscience
content: Bharat Bhushan Tiwari, 28, a man who said he was trying to change the system, died when police allegedly shot him down a dirt track in Bihar’s Bhojpur district, on June 17. Over a day, he had live-streamed the police surrounding his house, and recorded his anger at politicians’ “false promises”, while brandishing a gun. When news of his death went viral, outraged people protested and thousands attended a Maha Panchayat, reports Amit Bhelari. It prompted the State government to order a judicial enquiry, suspend police personnel, and register a murder case against at least two

title: Elephant chasers of Chalakudy are on constant vigil to avoid a ‘conflict’
content: In the Athirappilly forest area, teams of forest officials are constantly on the vigil, chasing away wild elephants that venture close to human habitations. An attempt to see firsthand how they operate in batches round the clock to avert human-wild elephant conflicts

title: Some Congress leaders signal possible rethink on Bidadi Township Project if farmers are unwilling to part with lands
content: The cautious position adopted by Karnataka Pradesh Congress Committee (KPCC) president B.K. Hariprasad and Home Minister Priyank Kharge on the proposed acquisition of agricultural land for the Bidadi Township Project, coupled with suggestions that the project may be shelved if farmers refuse to part with their holdings, is expected to strengthen the resolve of protesting farmers and provide fresh ammunition to the BJP-JD(S) alliance

title: Smart traffic system set to be launched by August, says official
content: Chennai will get 50 red-light violation detection cameras, 10 speed-violation systems and 58 traffic incident detection systems as a part of this.

title: Recruitment drive set to kickstart in Karnataka with number of jobs enhanced to 72,186
content: Public recruitment in Karnataka had been stalled since 2023 as a series of litigation over the reservation matters hit the processs

title: Another Bengaluru metro Purple Line snag at Cubbon Park Metro Station causes delays
content: According to the Bangalore Metro Rail Corporation Limited, the disruption occurred due to a problem with the train's door-closing mechanism. The issue led to a temporary halt in operations, affecting at least five subsequent train services and causing delays of nearly 20 minutes

title: Where are Kannada cinema’s next stars?
content: Kannada cinema still leans heavily on its tried-and-true male heroes and familiar storylines. It’s time for the industry to evolve and embrace a new generation of talent that reflects the vibrant, diverse audience it serves, writes Vivek M.V.

title: Watch: Passport, Aadhaar, voter ID — what proves your citizenship? | Above the Fold | 25.06.2026
content: In this episode, we examine the debate over whether a passport proves citizenship and what documents actually establish citizenship rights. We also cover the deadly earthquakes in Venezuela, NCERT’s new Class IX chapter on the Emergency and the political reactions it has sparked, and more.

title: Madras HC refuses to interfere with ex-Minister Geetha Jeevan’s acquittal from disproportionate assets case
content: Justice G.K. Ilanthiraiyan dismisses a petition filed by a third party to condone a delay of 839 days in preferring a revision against the acquittal

title: M.P. High Court closes defamation case against Rahul Gandhi
content: Decision comes a day after the Congress leader expressed regret over his statement allegedly against Union Agriculture Minister Shivraj Singh Chouhan’s son Kartikeya Singh Chouhan

title: 'She planned it, no he forced me': Twist in Pune realtor murder; Siya & Chetan turn on each other
content: A shocking murder case involving realtor Ketan Agarwal has taken a dramatic turn as his fiancée, Siya Goyal, and her friend, Chetan Chaudhary, reportedly blame each other for his death. Police investigations reveal conflicting accounts, with evidence currently pointing towards Goyal as the alleged mastermind behind the plot. The couple was set to marry later this year.

title: From baby to dog: Miracle rescues giving Venezuela hope after deadly earthquakes - watch
content: Amidst the devastation of twin earthquakes in Venezuela, remarkable rescues are offering glimmers of hope. Emergency workers and citizens are tirelessly searching for survivors beneath collapsed buildings, with a baby and a 12-year-old boy among those miraculously pulled to safety. These uplifting moments contrast sharply with the rising death toll and widespread destruction, as international aid begins to arrive.

title: Vaibhav Sooryavanshi on the verge of breaking Tendulkar's 36-year-old record
content: All eyes are on 15-year-old Vaibhav Sooryavanshi as India faces Ireland in Belfast. The teenage sensation, who had a stellar IPL 2026 season with Rajasthan Royals, could make history as India's youngest male international cricketer if he debuts.  His call-up breaks Sachin Tendulkar's long-standing record, fulfilling a lifelong dream for the young talent.

title: 'Is Rs 100 worth of our dead’: Mathura villagers ask after JCB damages 9 graves
content: In Mathura, the Kabristan Ahle Muslimeen committee is livid about the government's decision to provide only Rs 100 as compensation for each of the nine graves reportedly wrecked by a JCB. This minimal relief has been deemed disrespectful by grieving families who directly witnessed the vandalism. Locals claim that despite official assurances of compliance with protocols, there is a striking lack of empathy related to waqf land issues.

title: After pushing for China chip sales, Nvidia CEO tells shareholders: National security comes first
content: Nvidia CEO Jensen Huang declared national security paramount, even over business interests, especially concerning US export restrictions on AI chips to China. He assured shareholders that smuggled chips would lack crucial support, rendering them useless.  Huang also highlighted the growing demand for AI infrastructure, emphasizing Nvidia's cost-effective solutions and commitment to returning cash to investors.

title: Mumbai local murder: Accused came home drunk, cried after killing co-passenger
content: The recent stabbing on a Mumbai train has taken a toll on the accused's family, who claim he staggered home intoxicated and upset, disclosing the incident only the next morning. They assert that a trivial dispute over a train door spiraled into tragedy. Now, they're grappling with harsh public judgment and rampant speculation across social media as the story unfolds.

title: Palo Alto Networks CEO to companies cutting jobs: ‘Mass layoffs aren’t the solution’
content: While tech giants resort to mass layoffs for AI talent, Palo Alto Networks CEO Nikesh Arora advocates for a specialized hiring approach. He believes most employees lack AI savviness, making retraining difficult. Instead of firing, Palo Alto Networks uses natural attrition and innovative hackathon hiring to gradually build an AI-ready workforce, aiming to transform 20-25% of its team within a year.

title: Extortion case, marital troubles: What drove Bengaluru bizman to die by suicide at actor's flat
content: A 45-year-old businessman, previously arrested in a high-profile extortion case, was found dead by suicide in a Bengaluru apartment. Vaishak Upadhyaya, who was staying in the rented flat belonging to actress Krishi Thapanda, reportedly sent her distress messages about ongoing disputes and personal issues. He had been experiencing marital problems and was out on bail following his earlier arrest. An unnatural death report has been filed.

title: 'Grave mistake': Kuki-Zo Council apologises for killing 6 abducted Nagas in Manipur
content: In a significant development, the Kuki Zo Council has publicly apologized for the killing of six abducted Naga civilians, calling it a "grave mistake" driven by emotion. This remorse comes amidst ongoing ethnic tensions in Manipur, which have also flared between Kuki and Naga communities. The council chairman sought forgiveness, highlighting past retaliations and appealing for fair security operations and unimpeded access to essential supplies for affected Kuki-Zo villagers.

title: Bill Gates admits to the House panel that he had at least three extramarital affairs
content: Bill Gates revealed to Congress that Jeffrey Epstein attempted to leverage knowledge of Gates' extramarital affairs to re-establish contact after Gates sought to end their association. While no direct threats were made, Epstein allegedly used intermediaries for veiled warnings and explored blackmail tactics. Gates acknowledged past affairs, emphasizing Epstein's lack of involvement and that he only learned of them post-professional ties.

title: Maharashtra daycare centre: Toddler bites another 25 times
content: A shocking incident at a Chhatrapati Sambhajinagar daycare has led to a criminal case against its management. A 23-month-old child was allegedly bitten 25 times by another toddler after a staff member locked three children in a room unsupervised for 15 minutes. CCTV footage revealed the prolonged assault. The daycare has been ordered to shut down, with authorities investigating severe negligence.

title: 'Acid does not discriminate': Jharkhand HC raises compensation for male attack survivor; flags gender gap
content: Jharkhand High Court significantly increased acid attack survivor Rahul Kumar's compensation from Rs 3 lakh to Rs 15 lakh, deeming the initial amount 'meagre'. The court highlighted the disparity in compensation between male and female victims, urging the state to amend its scheme for equal treatment. Acid attacks inflict similar damage regardless of gender, the bench emphasized, calling for an end to discriminatory practices.

title: Bank holiday today: Will banks be closed on Muharram? Check city-wise list of closures
content: Several Indian cities will see bank closures on Friday, June 26, due to Muharram observances. However, branches in key locations like Ahmedabad, Chandigarh, and Jaipur will operate normally. While physical branches may be shut in places like Mumbai and New Delhi, digital banking services remain accessible. Customers are urged to verify local holiday schedules before visiting branches.

title: Belfast weather: Rain threat looms over Vaibhav Sooryavanshi's likely India debut
content: India's T20I opener against Ireland in Belfast faces potential rain disruptions, though conditions are expected to clear by the evening's scheduled start. All eyes are on 15-year-old Vaibhav Sooryavanshi, who could debut after a stellar IPL season. This series also marks Shreyas Iyer's captaincy debut for India in the T20 format.

title: Ujjain viral video shows dangerous mid-air stunt during Muharram procession
content: A shocking Muharram procession stunt in Ujjain saw a van hoisted 40 feet by a crane and detonated with firecrackers before thousands. While no one was injured, police have booked four individuals for endangering public safety. The dangerous display, which went viral online, has sparked serious concerns among locals regarding the potential for mass casualties from such reckless acts.

title: Before Vaibhav Sooryavanshi, who was the last teenager to play for India?
content: Sooryavanshi will be 15 years and 91 days old if he takes the field. Tendulkar was 16 years and 205 days old when he made his international debut against Pakistan in 1989.

title: Meet Shankh Mitra, Indian-origin CEO ranked second-highest paid in the world after Elon Musk
content: Shankh Mitra, an engineer originally from Kolkata, is making headlines as he takes the helm at Welltower, earning the title of the world's second-highest paid CEO. His remarkable compensation of $821 million in 2025, primarily through stock awards linked to the company's long-term success, exemplifies the rising trend in executive remuneration. Mitra's inspiring path from Jadavpur University to corporate stardom serves as a motivating story for many aspiring leaders.

title: Why Vaibhav Sooryavanshi will use a separate dressing room during Ireland T20Is
content: Teenage batting prodigy Vaibhav Sooryavanshi will use separate changing rooms during India's tours of Ireland and England due to UK child safeguarding laws. While he can join teammates for meetings and strategy, he'll change elsewhere. His parents are accompanying him throughout the tour, a special arrangement for the 15-year-old, ensuring his well-being under strict protocols.

title: Venezuela quake leaves 235 dead as rescue efforts intensify
content: Northern Venezuela grapples with devastation after twin earthquakes, with the death toll climbing past 235 and thousands feared affected. Rescue efforts are underway, though residents report slow official responses. International aid is pouring in, with the US authorizing significant assistance and numerous countries dispatching rescue teams. Despite widespread destruction, the nation's vital oil infrastructure remains largely intact.

title: Stock market holiday on Muharram: Will NSE, BSE remain closed for trading today?
content: Stock market investors, mark your calendars! The market will be closed on Friday, June 26, for Muharram. Trading operates on weekdays, excluding weekends and official holidays.  Remember the pre-open session from 9:00 am to 9:08 am, followed by the regular trading window from 9:15 am to 3:30 pm. Plan your trades wisely around these timings and upcoming 2026 holidays.

title: Crude oil stands at $75 as tankers head to Hormuz after four months of chaos
content: Crude oil prices dipped Friday as tanker traffic through the Strait of Hormuz improved, easing supply disruption fears. This comes despite recent tensions after a cargo ship near Oman was struck, with Iran reportedly firing at the vessel. While geopolitical risks linger, oil shipments through the strait have seen an uptick following a ceasefire.  Venezuela's oil production may also face challenges due to power outages following recent earthquakes.

title: Doug Martin cause of death: Fresh lawsuit reveals troubling new details
content: Doug Martin cause of death has come back into focus after a fresh lawsuit filed by the former NFL star’s parents revealed troubling new details about his final moments. The lawsuit alleges Doug Martin was restrained by Oakland police during a mental health crisis and did not receive timely medical care after becoming unresponsive. As the legal battle unfolds, Doug Martin’s family continues to seek answers while investigators have yet to release the official autopsy and toxicology reports.

title: FIFA WC: Plata's late strike stuns Germany as Ecuador seal knockout berth
content: Ecuador delivered an incredible shock to the football world by defeating four-time champion Germany 2-1, catapulting themselves into the knockout rounds of the World Cup. The late-game heroics of Gonzalo Plata, who scored the decisive goal, thrilled a record crowd. After an early strike from Germany, Ecuador's Nilson Angulo equalized, setting the stage for Plata's unforgettable winner that disrupted Germany's winning streak.

title: Nihangs clash with Uttarakhand Police at state border during march to Dehradun
content: A jatha of Nihang organisations was stopped by Uttarakhand Police at the Himachal-Uttarakhand border on Thursday while it was en route to Dehradun from Gurdwara Singh Shaheedan in Mohali, amid the ongoing dispute related to Karnaprayag.

title: 'All confusing': De Villiers tears into DC's 'wishy-washy' ownership as Pant returns
content: Pant returned to Delhi Capitals in a trade involving Kuldeep Yadav, ending a two-season stint with LSG and rejoining the franchise where he began his IPL career in 2016.

title: Massive explosion rocks Agartala apartment, several injured; bomb squad at spot
content: A powerful explosion rocked an Agartala apartment building on Ramnagar Road No. 4 Wednesday, injuring several residents and causing significant damage. The blast, originating from the first floor of NS Apartment, shattered windows and sent shockwaves through the area. Emergency services, including the Bomb Squad, are investigating the cause, with initial suspicions pointing towards a gas leak, though other possibilities are being explored. Injured individuals are receiving medical attention.

title: Ram Temple ‘donation theft’ case: FIR filed against 8, including cash counting in-charge
content: An FIR has been filed against eight individuals, including a cash-counting in-charge, following allegations of embezzlement of donations at the Ayodhya Ram Temple. A Special Investigation Team's preliminary report to the Chief Minister led to the registration of the case under charges of theft, criminal breach of trust, cheating, and conspiracy. The trust initiated a probe after financial irregularities surfaced, and police are now investigating the alleged diversion of funds.

title: CM Punk recalls terrifying WWE Brazil incident involving armed confrontation
content: CM Punk recounted a terrifying 2012 WWE incident in Brazil where Chris Jericho's desecration of the national flag led to authorities drawing guns on them. The situation escalated rapidly, with law enforcement demanding an apology and attempting to confiscate Jericho's passport. Punk highlighted Brazil's strict laws regarding flag disrespect, contrasting it with other nations. Meanwhile, WWE reportedly plans CM Punk's return for SummerSlam, potentially on SmackDown.

title: Taylor Swift’s reported text to Blake Lively sparks fresh speculation
content: Taylor Swift and actress Blake Lively seem to have experienced a lot of setbacks in their bond as rumors swirl about how the pop star has cut her off ahead of her wedding to Travis Kelce which is set to happen next week. Even though both Taylor Swift and Travis Kelce have maintained their fair share of distance from Blake Lively, the pop star’s texts to her have raised eyebrows.

title: Who is Nicolas Pépé’s fiancée? Inside Teanna Trump’s life and career
content: Villarreal winger Nicolas Pépé is reportedly planning to marry Teanna Trump, a former adult film star. The couple, who have been public with their relationship since late 2024, are rumored to be heading towards an engagement. Their high-profile romance has garnered significant attention online and within football circles.

title: Mahesh Dixit appointed Intelligence Bureau chief
content: He is among the Home Ministry officials who are engaged in talks with civil society leaders of Ladakh.

title: Rolex Watches, Silverware: In A 1st, Centre Puts Diplomatic Gifts For Auction
content: Ministry of External Affairs opens Toshakhana collection for public auction with nearly 300 diplomatic gifts on e-bidding

title: 'Push Her From Same Fort If Guilty': Pune Murder Accused Siya Goyal's Parents
content: Siya Goyal's parents said that if their daughter is found guilty, then she should be pushed from the same place where Ketan was thrown from.

title: Vijay Flags Off, Joins Anti-Drug Awareness Run At Chennai's Marina Beach
content: Dressed in tracks, sneakers and sunglasses, the Chief Minister drew loud cheers from participants, many of them youngsters, as he ran alongside them wearing a "Start Run, Stop Drugs" T-shirt.

title: Andhra Cop Seeks Entry Into Big Boss Telugu Show In Viral Video, Sparks Row
content: CC Nagarjuna Reddy, who is posted at Gadivemula police station, released a video wearing his uniform on social media, appealing to the makers of the popular reality show to consider him as a contestant.

title: Earthquakes Hit Venezuela, But Tremors Could Be Felt 14,000 Km Away In India
content: Power outages, damaged transport links and emergency restrictions at ports in Venezuela can slow cargo movements for days or even weeks.

title: Couple Found Dead Inside Car In Madhya Pradesh, 3 Phones Found 50 Feet Away
content: Preliminary investigation revealed that the couple had previously worked together in a private company in Indore, a link that police are examining closely.

title: Pentagon Sees Bigger Role For AI In Setting Military Targets
content: The revised targeting guidance is the latest example of the Pentagon's rush to accelerate adoption of AI, a technology that is already transforming battlefields in conflicts around the world.

title: 'No Voter Left Out': NCERT Backs SIR, Poll Body In Class 9 Textbook
content: The NCERT's social science book for Class 9 describes the EC as an autonomous body that ensures "free and fair elections".

title: Weather Today LIVE: Monsoon Nears Delhi-NCR, Rain Likely Over Next Few Days
content: Weather Updates Today: The India Meteorological Department (IMD) has forecast light to moderate rainfall, thunderstorms, gusty winds and cloudy skies across Delhi-NCR and several parts of North India over the coming week.

title: Ship Attack In Strait Of Hormuz Halts UN Evacuation Plan For Trapped Sailors
content: The New York Times and other US media reported that Iran struck a container ship in the strait, and the Iranian agency that claims to regulate traffic there issued a warning after the incident.

title: How Phone Alerts Warn Millions Seconds Before Earthquakes
content: Several countries have Early Earthquake Warnings, including the United States, Mexico, Japan, Turkey, Romania, China, Italy and Taiwan, according to the US Geological Survey.

title: Man Kills Wife In Delhi After Dispute Over His Extramarital Affair: Cops
content: A 30-year-old woman was allegedly shot dead by her husband in south Delhi's Sangam Vihar area, with the accused fleeing the spot after the incident, police said on Friday.

title: 'Ketan Agarwal Said I Won't Be Able To Escape Marriage': Pune Murder Accused To Cops
content: According to sources, Siya told the authorities that she was in a relationship with Chetan Chaudhary.

title: US-India Trade Deal: Piyush Goyal Signals Next Move After Two Days Of Talks
content: India-US Trade Deal: India is seeking greater access for its labour-intensive exports, while the US wants wider access for agricultural products.

title: Bengaluru Metro Ops Hit Again, Snag On Purple Line Causes 17-Minute Delay
content: The BMRCL said the technical issue was quickly fixed by the Operations and Maintenance teams, and normal metro services have now been fully restored across the Purple Line.

title: In A First For UK Monarch, King Charles Reveals Tax Bill, $40 Million
content: UK monarchs are legally exempt from paying certain taxes, though they have paid some duties voluntarily for decades, and they also have no obligation to disclose their private tax bills.

title: Two Madhya Pradesh Karate Instructors Sentenced To Life For Gang-Raping Minor Girl
content: In the complaint, the minor had alleged that the two instructors raped her not only in Guna, but also in other parts of the country as well as abroad.

title: Venezuela Earthquakes Kill 235, Over 1,500 Injured
content: More than 1,500 people were injured as the earth shook and roared in what many Venezuelans called a terrifying display of nature's raw power.

title: Passport was not citizenship proof even in 1967 Act: MEA
content: official sources say the statement was merely a reiteration of what has been followed by the authorities over last several decades . the passport has never been a proof of citizenship, an official said . former foreign secretary Nirupama Rao said the position that a passport is a travel document and not a document of citizenship is “legally correct”

content: The TOI Real Estate Desk is a focused team of seasoned journalists and market watchers dedicated to decoding the ever-evolving property landscape for The Times of India readers. title: ‘Pit bulls guarded us, we were beaten like dogs’: Rescued bonded labourers from UP recall horror
content: A journalist with two decades of experience, having worked with major news organisations such as NDTV, Times Now, and others. As a Principal Correspondent covering in West Uttar Pradesh, he reports on crime, courts, politics, human-interest stories, and minority issues. His coverage includes key institutions such as Darul Uloom Deoband and Aligarh Muslim University, as well as districts including Agra, Aligarh, and Muzaffarnagar. Read More

title: Passport fees hike: What applicants will pay for normal, tatkal services from July 1
content: The TOI News Desk comprises a dedicated and tireless team of journalists who operate around the clock to deliver the most current and comprehensive news and updates to the readers of The Times of India worldwide. With an unwavering commitment to excellence in journalism, our team is at the forefront of gathering, verifying, and presenting breaking news, in-depth analysis, and insightful reports on a wide range of topics. The TOI News Desk is your trusted source for staying informed and connected to the ever-evolving global landscape, ensuring that our readers are equipped with the latest developments that matter most." Read More

title: Not passport, Aadhaar or voter ID: Which document actually proves you are an Indian citizen?
content: Aastha Jha is a writer at The Times of India, covering politics. She also writes on business, global affairs, social issues, with a focus on policy, governance and their impact on everyday lives. Her work brings together reportage and analysis, tracking developments in India and abroad while examining the broader social and economic currents shaping public discourse. Read More
